name: Christmas Shortbread
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C).
2. Cream butter, sugar, and vanilla until light and fluffy.
3. Mix flour and salt, then beat into the butter mixture until thoroughly blended, forming a soft dough.
4. To make bars: Dust work surface with flour. Pat dough into a rectangle about 1/2 inch thick, 2 inches wide, and 17 inches long. Use a knife or fluted pastry cutter to cut into 1-inch bars. Transfer bars to an ungreased cookie sheet, leaving 1 inch between bars. Prick each bar with a fork several times.
5. Bake for 20-25 minutes, until lightly colored. Do not overbake; the bars should not brown. Let cool on the cookie sheet for one minute, then transfer to wire racks to finish cooling.
6. To make wedges: Dust hands and an ungreased cookie sheet with flour. Pat dough into a ball. Press ball into a flat disk about 1/2 inch thick and 7 inches in diameter on the cookie sheet. Mark disk into 8 wedges, cutting about halfway through the dough, and prick dough all over with a fork. Bake for 30 minutes, then remove from the oven. Cut shortbread on marked lines. Return to oven for five more minutes. Let wedges cool on the cookie sheet for one minute, then transfer to wire racks to finish cooling.
7. Nut Shortbread: Add 1/4 cup finely chopped toasted pecans, almonds, walnuts, or hazelnuts to flour mixture.
8. Chocolate Chip Shortbread: Add 1/4 cup miniature semisweet chocolate chips to flour mixture.
9. Orange-Chocolate Chip Shortbread: Add 1 teaspoon grated orange rind and 1/4 cup miniature semisweet chocolate chips to flour mixture.
10. Lemon Shortbread: Add 1 teaspoon grated lemon rind to flour mixture.
